concerns.text: Task T013 ('Implement code/data/download.py... validate source title-token overlap') violates the data-flow dependency on the external Reference-Validator Agent mandated by Constitution Principle II. The task description implies the download script performs the validation logic itself, but the plan.md and constitution require this to be a separate agent call. T013 must be split or rephrased to 'Call Reference-Validator Agent' rather than 'Implement validation logic', otherwise the implementation will violate the architectural contract before execution begins.

Task T023 ('Implement emcee runner... with Gelman-Rubin convergence check') creates a semantic ordering conflict with FR-003. FR-003 states the run continues 'OR until Gelman-Rubin < 1.01, whichever requires MORE steps'. T023's description implies a fixed 5000-step limit with a check, which contradicts the 'whichever requires more' logic. If the check fails at step 5000, the task as written stops, violating the requirement to continue. The task must explicitly include logic to extend steps beyond 5000 if convergence is not met, or the spec/plan must be amended to hard-cap at 5000.

Task T015 ('Implement covariance matrix construction') and T022 ('Implement log-likelihood function with block-diagonal/banded covariance') contain a hidden dependency contradiction. T015 implies constructing a 'full' matrix (per FR-002), while T022 and plan.md explicitly plan for a 'block-diagonal/banded' approximation. If T015 produces a full matrix, T022's assumption of a banded structure may fail or require a transformation step not listed. A task to 'Convert full covariance to banded approximation' or a decision task to 'Select covariance strategy (full vs banded)' is missing between T015 and T022 to resolve this semantic gap.

The test tasks (T010-T012) are listed BEFORE the implementation tasks (T013-T017) in the visual hierarchy, which is good for TDD, but the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section states 'Tests (if included) MUST be written and FAIL before implementation'. However, the task IDs (T010 < T013) suggest they are sequential. If the team intends to run T010-T012 in parallel with T013-T017 (as implied by the [P] tags on tests but not on implementation), the dependency graph is ambiguous. Clarify if T013-T017 depend on T010-T012 failing, or if they are independent parallel tracks.
